content:
# 1 Corinthians Chapter 10 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (א), 4th century CE | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/1CO_010_page_001.jpg

1. For I do not want you to be ignorant, brothers, that the fathers of us were all under the cloud, and all passed through the sea,

2. and all were baptized into Moshe in the cloud and in the sea,

3. and all ate the same spiritual food,

4. and all drank the same spiritual drink; for they were drinking from a spiritual rock following [them], and the rock was the Christos.

5. But with the majority of them God was not pleased, for they were struck down in the wilderness.

6. And these [things] became examples for us, that we might not be desirers of evil [things], just as those also desired.

7. Neither become idolaters, as some of them [did]; as it has been written, "The people sat down to eat and drink and stood up to play."

8. Neither let us commit sexual immorality, as some of them committed sexual immorality, and fell in one day twenty-three thousand.

9. Neither let us test the Christos, as some of them tested, and were destroyed by the serpents.

10. Neither grumble, as some of them grumbled, and were destroyed by the destroyer.

11. And these [things] happened to them as examples, and were written for the admonition of us, upon whom the ends of the ages have come.

12. So then, the [one] thinking to stand, let him watch lest he fall.

13. A temptation has not seized you except [what is] human. And faithful [is] God, who will not allow you to be tempted beyond what you are able, but will make with the temptation also the way out, so that you are able to bear [it].

14. Therefore, beloved of me, flee from the idolatry.

15. As to wise [ones] I speak; judge yourselves what I say.

16. The cup of the blessing which we bless, is it not a sharing in the blood of the Christos? The bread which we break, is it not a sharing in the body of the Christos?

17. Because [there is] one bread, we the many are one body; for we all share from the one bread.

18. Look at Israel according to [the] flesh: are not the [ones] eating the sacrifices sharers in the altar?

19. What then do I say? That [food] sacrificed to an idol is anything, or that an idol is anything?

20. No, but that what they sacrifice, they sacrifice to demons and not to God. And I do not want you to become sharers with the demons.

21. You are not able to drink [the] cup of [the] Lord and [the] cup of demons. You are not able to share in [the] table of [the] Lord and [the] table of demons.

22. Or do we provoke the Lord to jealousy? Are we stronger than he?

23. "All [things] are permitted," but not all [things] are beneficial. "All [things] are permitted," but not all [things] build up.

24. Let no one seek the [thing] of himself, but the [thing] of the other.

25. Everything sold in [the] meat market eat, examining nothing on account of the conscience;

26. for "the earth [is] the Lord's and the fullness of it."

27. If any of the unbelievers invites you and you wish to go, everything set before you eat, examining nothing on account of the conscience.

28. But if anyone should say to you, "This is sacred offering," do not eat, on account of the [one] having pointed [it] out and on account of the conscience —

29. the conscience, I say, not the [one] of yourself but the [one] of the other. For why is the freedom of me judged by another conscience?

30. If I partake with thankfulness, why am I spoken evil of concerning that for which I give thanks?

31. Therefore, whether you eat or drink or whatever you do, do all [things] for [the] glory of God.

32. Become without offense both to Ioudaioi and to Greeks and to the assembly of God,

33. just as I also please all [people] in all [things], not seeking the profit of myself but the [profit] of the many, that they may be saved.

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **v.4 — "the rock was the Christos":** ἡ πέτρα δὲ ἦν ὁ Χριστός — Paul identifies Christ with the wilderness rock of Exodus 17:6 and Numbers 20:11. The "following" rock reflects a Jewish midrashic tradition (preserved in Tosefta Sukkah 3:11) that the rock traveled with Israel through the wilderness.

2. **v.8 — "twenty-three thousand":** Numbers 25:9 says "twenty-four thousand." The discrepancy may be due to Paul counting differently (one day vs. total), using a variant tradition, or citing from memory.

3. **v.9 — "test the Christ":** Sinaiticus reads τὸν Χριστόν ("the Christ"), not τὸν κύριον ("the Lord"). This is significant — Paul identifies Christ as the divine figure whom Israel tested in the wilderness, attributing pre-existence to Christ.

4. **v.16 — κοινωνία:** "Sharing," "participation," "communion." The Eucharistic cup and bread create real participation in Christ's blood and body. This is Paul's earliest eucharistic theology.

5. **v.20 — "they sacrifice to demons":** Paul quotes Deuteronomy 32:17 (LXX). While idols are nothing (v.19), the spiritual reality behind idol worship is demonic. Paul holds both truths simultaneously.

6. **v.26 — Psalm 24:1 (LXX 23:1):** The earth belongs to the Lord — therefore all food is intrinsically clean. The citation grounds freedom of conscience in creation theology.

— EMET, AI Translator
